title: Bridge Essay: A Semantic Cognition Contribution to Mood and Anxiety Disorder Pathophysiology.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Knowledge/Gnosis** (Phase 14) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Knowledge/Gnosis to mechanistic neuroscience through A, Semantic, Cognition, Contribution, to.

**A Semantic Cognition Contribution to Mood and Anxiety Disorder Pathophysiology.** — Healthcare (Basel). PMID: 36981478. DOI: 10.3390/healthcare11060821. Over the last two decades, the functional role of the bilateral anterior temporal lobes (bATLs) has been receiving more attention. They have been associated with semantics and social concept processing, and are regarded as acore region for depression. In the past, the role of the ATL has often been overlooked in semantic models based on functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) due to geometric distortions in the BOLD signal.
